_comment: OC Pledge example — SLA commitment. A platform provider commits to 99.9% uptime to a customer. Resolution is bilateral: only the named counterparty can sign the kept/broken outcome. A pre-named vote_resolves dispute mechanism gives the swearer recourse if the counterparty stays silent or publishes a contradictory outcome. The bond is 2.5M sats × 365 days unspent — a real economic commitment that never moves. Enforcement is by public-record exposure: a broken pledge attaches to bc1qcarol's address forever. The protocol cannot seize the bond. To run this example: (1) negotiate the SLA terms with the counterparty out-of-band (OC Lock works well for this), (2) BIP-322-sign the canonical message, (3) publish to Nostr kind-30078 d-tag oc-pledge:<id>, (4) at deadline, the counterparty publishes a counterparty_signs outcome envelope with their BIP-322 signature.
_flow: PROTOCOL.md Flow 2 — A SLA pledge between two service providers (counterparty_signs)
_dispute: vote_resolves with maintainers council poll if outcome contradicted
proposition: I will deliver service-level uptime of at least 99.9 percent to bc1qcounter0000000000000000000000000000000 during the window 2026-05-01 to 2026-06-01.

sig.value: <base64 BIP-322 signature by bc1qcarol over hex pledge_id>
